---
status: draft
shortDescription: "If code could throw an exception, you must surround that code with a try/catch block"
concepts:
  - Exception
  - ControlFlow
related:
# - id: LiteralNoExpression
#   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
# - pl: JavaScript
#   id: TryCatchMandatory
# - pl: Python
#   id: TryCatchMandatory
spec:
# - id: jls13
#   sections: ["11.8"]
plVersion:
# from: jls8
# to: jls14
---
If code can throw an exception, that code has to be inside a try/catch block. The exception can be propagated to the calling method by throwing it from the catch block, e.g., like this: `try { c = a[i]; } catch (ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException ex) { throw ex; }`.

frontmatter.shortDescription: If code could throw an exception, you must surround that code with a try/catch block
